J1.basis: "Pivot note (Confirmed from repo + GSC): this site previously operated as InboxZap... while the on-page content and nav now describe Lumina. gsc/Queries.csv and gsc/Pages.csv are entirely legacy InboxZap-era demand"; H1 "Never take meeting notes again", body "Lumina is the AI meeting assistant for teams"
J1.rationale: Explicitly and correctly identifies the pivot: live site = AI meeting assistant, GSC = legacy InboxZap demand, repo metadata partially stale, with the brand-mismatch implication stated clearly. Falls just short of anchor_10 because it doesn't frame forward-looking recommendations around it — the doc is diagnostic.

J2.basis: "GSC's Pages.csv export shows 12 distinct /blog/post-N URLs (post-1 through post-12) carrying 13,700 combined impressions and 6 total clicks, all with positions 42–58 — historically indexed, now dead."
J2.rationale: Cites real aggregate GSC numbers and flags the 12 legacy posts as dead, but treats them as one undifferentiated block — no asset-by-asset (or tiered) harvest/redirect/keep/sunset dispositions and no per-post impression breakdown.

J3.basis: "This is a upstream_dependency_unreachable + page_renders_empty critical: the blog is permanently broken"; brand-mismatch flagged as critical
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes only by omission — it neither chases inbox-cleanup content nor advocates torching equity — but it never articulates the tension or lands a transition path (redirect salvageable equity, build the meeting-assistant cluster, sequence the move); it stays purely diagnostic.

J1.basis: "Type: SaaS — AI meeting assistant" ... "Pivot note (Confirmed from repo + GSC): this site previously operated as InboxZap ... app/layout.tsx metadata still serves the old InboxZap copy ... gsc/Queries.csv and gsc/Pages.csv are entirely legacy InboxZap-era demand"
J1.rationale: The entrant explicitly nails the pivot: live/on-page product = AI meeting assistant, head metadata = stale InboxZap, and GSC = entirely legacy inbox-cleanup demand, framing it as the core reality. Just short of a 10 because the framing is diagnostic rather than threaded through every forward recommendation.

J2.basis: "GSC's Pages.csv export shows 12 distinct /blog/post-N URLs (post-1 through post-12) carrying 13,700 combined impressions and 6 total clicks, all with positions 42–58 — historically indexed, now dead."
J2.rationale: It cites the aggregate legacy numbers and confirms the 12 posts now 404, but never walks the inventory asset-by-asset or assigns any harvest/redirect/keep/sunset disposition to individual posts — it's evidence-gathering, not a migration plan.

J3.basis: "12 /blog/post-N URLs with real impressions that now 404" and "Google will index/snippet the InboxZap copy while users clicking through land on an unrelated product."
J3.rationale: It correctly avoids doubling down on dead inbox-cleanup content and flags the lost legacy equity, so it doesn't fall fully into either failure mode, but it never articulates the tension as a choice or lands a defensible middle path (what to redirect vs. rebuild, how to sequence the transition).

J1.basis: "Pivot note (Confirmed from repo + GSC): this site previously operated as InboxZap... `app/layout.tsx` metadata... still serves the old InboxZap copy on every page, while the on-page content and nav now describe Lumina. `gsc/Queries.csv` and `gsc/Pages.csv` are entirely legacy InboxZap-era demand"
J1.rationale: Explicitly nails the pivot: live site/nav = Lumina meeting assistant, head metadata = stale InboxZap, GSC = entirely legacy inbox demand, and states the implication (Google snippets InboxZap while users land on an unrelated product). Essentially anchor_10.

J2.basis: "GSC's `Pages.csv` export shows 12 distinct `/blog/post-N` URLs (post-1 through post-12) carrying 13,700 combined impressions and 6 total clicks, all with positions 42–58 — historically indexed, now dead."
J2.rationale: Identifies the 12-post inventory and cites real aggregate GSC numbers, but stops at 'now dead' — no per-asset (or tiered) harvest/redirect/keep/sunset dispositions, leaving all the actual migration work undone.

J3.basis: "This is a upstream_dependency_unreachable + page_renders_empty critical: the blog is permanently broken" / brand-mismatch findings — no transition strategy section present
J3.rationale: It avoids both failure modes (never chases inbox content, never says torch everything), but it's a pure diagnostic audit — it never articulates the equity-vs-growth tension or sequences a bridge from legacy authority to the meeting-assistant positioning.
